channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hundreds of national security officials, ex-Cabinet members, Gold Star families endorse Trump
item.description: More than 400 national security and foreign policy officials who have worked in previous GOP administrations endorsed Donald Trump for president on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buttigieg's message on restricting civilian drones near Hurricane Helene damage prompts outcry, clarification
item.description: Biden administration Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg says civilian drone activity near Hurricane Helene disaster sites impedes emergency response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gallego rakes in close to $22M in third quarter for Arizona Senate race
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) raised close to $22 million in the third quarter of fundraising for this year in the Arizona Senate race, his campaign announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photo shows U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler wearing blackface at college Halloween party in 2006
item.description: U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler said he was sorry after the New York Times obtained photos of him wearing blackface about two decades ago at a college Halloween costume party where he dressed as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taxpayers in 24 states will be able to file their returns directly with the IRS in 2025
item.description: The IRS is expanding its program that allows people to file their taxes directly with the agency for free.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, City Council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the mayor’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What's next after prosecutors reveal new evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith has provided a road map for how prosecutors hope to prove their case charging Donald Trump with an illegal scheme to overturn his 2020 election loss — if it ever gets to trial.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Debate performance gives Vance 2028 White House boost
item.description: Sen. JD Vance’s (R-Ohio) debate performance is giving him a shot in the arm to become a leading 2028 presidential contender no matter what happens in November between former President Trump and Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Valadao, Salas critique each other's records during CA-22 debate
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- The hour-long debate featured a deep dive into local, state and federal issues, as well as critiques on each other's records as lawmakers. One of the most highly-contested races nationwide, CA-22 is a difficult-to-predict toss-up.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man convicted for dozens of threats against former Speaker McCarthy over Chinese balloons
item.description: A man from Montana was convicted by a federal jury this week on the charges of threatening former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy over the course of more than 100 phone calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In excerpt from new memoir, Melania Trump says women have the ‘right to choose’ abortion
item.description: The former first lady says she is a longtime supporter of abortion rights. Her memoir is coming out a year after former President Donald Trump said he was "able to kill Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: 5 key details in special counsel Jack Smith's Trump election case filing
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith argues in a new court filing that former President Donald Trump is not immune from prosecution for his conduct immediately after the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Valadao, Salas spar over issues in California debate
item.description: David Valadao and Rudy Salas debated over issues voters said are of the most importance to them during a debate.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting begins in downtown Chicago
item.description: Early voting for the November election begins Thursday in downtown Chicago, with DuPage County seeing nearly double the amount of voters who turned out early in 2020, and early voting in all 50 wards opens up next Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Influential prophesizing pastors believe reelecting Trump is a win in the war of angels and demons
item.description: COUNCIL BLUFFS, Iowa (AP) — Thousands sang, cheered and prayed as multiple preachers declared Donald Trump to be God’s favored candidate to defeat what one called the “forces of darkness.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump credits an immigration chart for saving his life and what the graphic is missing
item.description: Ever since the assassination attempt at his rally in Pennsylvania, Donald Trump has professed a unique fondness for a bar chart he credits for saving his life. Trump was addressing the crowd in Butler about illegal immigration and reviewing a chart that detailed U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kamala Harris, Liz Cheney to stump at birthplace of Republican Party
item.description: 2024 Election development: Vice President Kamala Harris will team up with leading anti-Trump Republican Liz Cheney at the town in battleground Wisconsin that claims to be the birthplace of the GOP
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris-Walz campaign kicks off a nationwide effort to reach Native voters
item.description: Tribal leaders from across the country showed their support for the democratic ticket at Mystic Lake Event Center in Prior Lake on Wednesday. Native American civic leaders say the Harris-Walz campaign is prepared to support issues that impact Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Why Vance easily beat Walz in debate, softening his image in the process
item.description: White House hopeful JD Vance was the undisputed champion of CBS News' Vice Presidential Debate Tuesday night, according to Fox News' Howard Kurtz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Haunted by 2016, some Michigan Democrats worry that Harris remains ill-defined in swing state
item.description: EASTPOINTE, Mich. (AP) — Bill Clinton stood with Michigan Democratic Rep. Debbie Dingell outside a suburban Detroit grocery store two days before the 2016 election when it was clear to both that Hillary Clinton would lose the state.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Braun, McCormick trade attacks, discuss issues during first Indiana governor debate
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- While there were many issues discussed during the first debate between Indiana's top two gubernatorial records, much of the debate served as attacks on each other's records, as well as their respective running mates. On Wednesday evening, U.S. Senator Mike Braun, R-Ind.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance, Walz address school shootings
item.description: Walz said the solution is more research into gun violence. Vance said we have to increase security in our schools.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Texas Senate race toward Democrats
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted Texas’s Senate race from "likely Republican" to "lean Republican," signaling momentum for Rep. Colin Allred's (D) challenge against incumbent Sen. Ted Cruz (R) in the red state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation
